assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a recognised monetary item denominated in a foreign currency.
The question concerns designation of foreign currency risk as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 hedge accounting.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a top-level hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.
